{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Finance Officer (Mid Level)",
  "description": "Elevare Human Solutions Limited is a forward-thinking HR consulting and workforce solutions company committed to helping organizations build high-performing, people-driven businesses. We partner with companies across industries to deliver strategic and operational HR support that drives productivity, compliance, and sustainable growth. Our core focus areas include talent acquisition, workforce planning, HR advisory, employer branding, and end-to-end people management solutions. We also provide tailored staffing support and operational workforce solutions through our cleaning and facility support services subsidiary, enabling businesses to maintain efficient and well-managed environments.

At Elevare, we understand that people are the most critical asset in any organization. Our approach is rooted in aligning business objectives with the right talent, systems, and culture to create measurable impact. We combine deep HR expertise with practical execution to support both startups and established organizations in building scalable and resilient teams. We are driven by excellence, professionalism, and a strong commitment to delivering value. Our goal is to elevate businesses through strategic human capital solutions that empower growth and long-term success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Finance Officer (Mid Level)

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Job Summary


	The Senior Finance Officer (Mid-Level) is responsible for supporting the Company&rsquo;s financial management activities, including financial reporting, project accounting, treasury operations, statutory compliance, and budget monitoring.
	The role is designed for a seasoned finance professional with strong technical competence and practical experience in project-based accounting within engineering, procurement, installation, and commissioning (EPIC) environments.
	The incumbent will play a critical role in ensuring the integrity of financial records, monitoring project profitability, and providing timely financial information to support operational and strategic decision-making.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting and General Ledger Management:


	Prepare monthly management accounts and supporting schedules.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date accounting records.
	Process journal entries and general ledger adjustments.
	Perform bank, vendor, and balance sheet reconciliations.
	Support year-end closing and audit processes.


Project Finance and Cost Monitoring:


	Track project expenditures against approved budgets.
	Prepare project cost reports and profitability analysis.
	Monitor revenue recognition in line with contractual milestones.
	Investigate and report budget variances.
	Support project managers with financial insights and cost controls.


Treasury and Cash Management:


	Prepare weekly cash flow reports and forecasts.
	Process approved payments to vendors and staff.
	Monitor bank balances and liquidity requirements.
	Liaise with banks on transfers, account maintenance, and related matters.


Accounts Payable and Receivable:


	Review invoices and payment requests for completeness and accuracy.
	Prepare customer invoices and monitor collections.
	Follow up on overdue receivables.
	Reconcile customer and supplier statements.


Budgeting and Forecasting:


	Assist in the preparation of annual budgets and rolling forecasts.
	Monitor departmental and project spend against budget.


Tax and Regulatory Compliance:


	Prepare and process statutory remittances, including PAYE, VAT, WHT, Pension, NHF, and NSITF.
	Ensure timely filing of tax returns and regulatory submissions.
	Liaise with relevant regulatory authorities when required.


Internal Control and Compliance:


	Ensure adherence to company financial policies and approval limits.
	Identify control gaps and recommend improvements.
	Support implementation of robust financial controls.


Management Reporting:


	Prepare ad hoc financial analyses and reports.
	Provide financial data to support management decisions.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timeliness and accuracy of monthly management reports.
	Accuracy of reconciliations and financial records.
	Compliance with statutory remittance deadlines.
	Receivables collection performance.


Educational Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance,
	ACA, ACCA, or equivalent professional certification is required.


Experience Requirements:


	4&ndash;6 years of relevant finance and accounting experience.
	Experience in engineering, construction, oil and gas, procurement, or project-based industries is strongly preferred.
	Proven experience in project accounting and statutory compliance within Nigeria.


Technical Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of IFRS and Nigerian tax regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (advanced level).
	Experience with accounting software such as Sage, QuickBooks, SAP, or similar ERP systems.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: recruitment4jen@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-14",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-11",

      "maxValue": 600000,

Senior Finance Officer (Mid Level) Job at Elevare Human Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Elevare Human Solutions Limited is a forward-thinking HR consulting and workforce solutions company committed to helping organizations build high-performing, people-driven businesses. We partner with companies across industries to deliver strategic and operational HR support that drives productivity, compliance, and sustainable growth. Our core focus areas include talent acquisition, workforce planning, HR advisory, employer branding, and end-to-end people management solutions. We also provide tailored staffing support and operational workforce solutions through our cleaning and facility support services subsidiary, enabling businesses to maintain efficient and well-managed environments.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At Elevare, we understand that people are the most critical asset in any organization. Our approach is rooted in aligning business objectives with the right talent, systems, and culture to create measurable impact. We combine deep HR expertise with practical execution to support both startups and established organizations in building scalable and resilient teams. We are driven by excellence, professionalism, and a strong commitment to delivering value. Our goal is to elevate businesses through strategic human capital solutions that empower growth and long-term success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Finance Officer (Mid Level)

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Job Summary


	The Senior Finance Officer (Mid-Level) is responsible for supporting the Company’s financial management activities, including financial reporting, project accounting, treasury operations, statutory compliance, and budget monitoring.
	The role is designed for a seasoned finance professional with strong technical competence and practical experience in project-based accounting within engineering, procurement, installation, and commissioning (EPIC) environments.
	The incumbent will play a critical role in ensuring the integrity of financial records, monitoring project profitability, and providing timely financial information to support operational and strategic decision-making.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting and General Ledger Management:


	Prepare monthly management accounts and supporting schedules.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date accounting records.
	Process journal entries and general ledger adjustments.
	Perform bank, vendor, and balance sheet reconciliations.
	Support year-end closing and audit processes.


Project Finance and Cost Monitoring:


	Track project expenditures against approved budgets.
	Prepare project cost reports and profitability analysis.
	Monitor revenue recognition in line with contractual milestones.
	Investigate and report budget variances.
	Support project managers with financial insights and cost controls.


Treasury and Cash Management:


	Prepare weekly cash flow reports and forecasts.
	Process approved payments to vendors and staff.
	Monitor bank balances and liquidity requirements.
	Liaise with banks on transfers, account maintenance, and related matters.


Accounts Payable and Receivable:


	Review invoices and payment requests for completeness and accuracy.
	Prepare customer invoices and monitor collections.
	Follow up on overdue receivables.
	Reconcile customer and supplier statements.


Budgeting and Forecasting:


	Assist in the preparation of annual budgets and rolling forecasts.
	Monitor departmental and project spend against budget.


Tax and Regulatory Compliance:


	Prepare and process statutory remittances, including PAYE, VAT, WHT, Pension, NHF, and NSITF.
	Ensure timely filing of tax returns and regulatory submissions.
	Liaise with relevant regulatory authorities when required.


Internal Control and Compliance:


	Ensure adherence to company financial policies and approval limits.
	Identify control gaps and recommend improvements.
	Support implementation of robust financial controls.


Management Reporting:


	Prepare ad hoc financial analyses and reports.
	Provide financial data to support management decisions.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timeliness and accuracy of monthly management reports.
	Accuracy of reconciliations and financial records.
	Compliance with statutory remittance deadlines.
	Receivables collection performance.


Educational Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance,
	ACA, ACCA, or equivalent professional certification is required.


Experience Requirements:


	4–6 years of relevant finance and accounting experience.
	Experience in engineering, construction, oil and gas, procurement, or project-based industries is strongly preferred.
	Proven experience in project accounting and statutory compliance within Nigeria.


Technical Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of IFRS and Nigerian tax regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (advanced level).
	Experience with accounting software such as Sage, QuickBooks, SAP, or similar ERP systems.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "General Manager (Construction Equipment & Machinery)",
  "description": "PCP Construction Nigeria Limited, a solution to a powerful future, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Construction Equipment &amp; Machinery)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Brief


	The General Manager of the construction equipment and machinery leasing company will be responsible for overseeing the company&#39;s operations, ensuring that all machinery and equipment are rented and leased efficiently, and managing the company&#39;s inventory.
	The GM will also oversee management and maintenance schedules.
	The General Manager (GM) will oversee the overall operations, strategy, and profitability of the construction equipment and machinery leasing company.


Key Responsibilities


	Strategic Oversight: Ensuring that all machinery and equipment are available, rented, and leased efficiently, meeting the needs of clientele.
	Operational Efficiency: Collaborating with various departments to manage inventory, oversee maintenance schedules, and implement safety and regulatory compliance measures.
	Customer Service Excellence: Building lasting customer relationships and providing excellent customer service.
	Leadership and Staff Management: Training and managing staff, optimizing logistics, and fostering a positive work culture.
	Operational Management: Oversee day-to-day operations including equipment leasing, maintenance schedules, logistics, and customer service.
	Financial Oversight: Prepare budgets, monitor financial performance, manage costs, and ensure revenue targets are met.
	Team Management: Lead, mentor, and motivate staff across sales, operations, and technical teams to achieve organizational goals.
	Compliance &amp; Safety: Ensure adherence to industry regulations, safety standards, and environmental policies.
	Reporting &amp; Analysis: Provide regular performance reports to the board, analyze market trends, and recommend improvements.


Performance Metrics


	Fleet utilization rate
	Revenue growth and profitability
	Customer satisfaction scores
	Operational efficiency (downtime reduction, maintenance costs)
	Employee engagement and retention


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in business administration, engineering, or related field (MBA preferred).
	Minimum of 10 years of experience in equipment leasing, construction, or heavy machinery industry, with at least 5 years in a senior management role.
	Proven track record of driving business growth and operational efficiency.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Leadership &amp; People Management: Ability to inspire and manage diverse teams.
	Industry Knowledge: Strong understanding of construction equipment, machinery operations, and leasing practices.
	Financial Acumen: Expertise in budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis.
	Negotiation &amp; Communication: Skilled in contract negotiation, stakeholder engagement, and clear communication.
	Problem-Solving: Ability to handle operational challenges and deliver practical solutions.
	Technology Proficiency: Familiarity with fleet management software, ERP systems, and digital leasing platforms.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: humancapital@pcpnigeria.com using &#39;&#39;General Manager (Construction Equipment &amp; Machinery)&#39;&#39; as the subject of the mail.",

General Manager (Construction Equipment & Machinery) Job at PCP Construction Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        PCP Construction Nigeria Limited, a solution to a powerful future, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Construction Equipment & Machinery)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Brief
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The General Manager of the construction equipment and machinery leasing company will be responsible for overseeing the company's operations, ensuring that all machinery and equipment are rented and leased efficiently, and managing the company's inventory.
	The GM will also oversee management and maintenance schedules.
	The General Manager (GM) will oversee the overall operations, strategy, and profitability of the construction equipment and machinery leasing company.


Key Responsibilities


	Strategic Oversight: Ensuring that all machinery and equipment are available, rented, and leased efficiently, meeting the needs of clientele.
	Operational Efficiency: Collaborating with various departments to manage inventory, oversee maintenance schedules, and implement safety and regulatory compliance measures.
	Customer Service Excellence: Building lasting customer relationships and providing excellent customer service.
	Leadership and Staff Management: Training and managing staff, optimizing logistics, and fostering a positive work culture.
	Operational Management: Oversee day-to-day operations including equipment leasing, maintenance schedules, logistics, and customer service.
	Financial Oversight: Prepare budgets, monitor financial performance, manage costs, and ensure revenue targets are met.
	Team Management: Lead, mentor, and motivate staff across sales, operations, and technical teams to achieve organizational goals.
	Compliance & Safety: Ensure adherence to industry regulations, safety standards, and environmental policies.
	Reporting & Analysis: Provide regular performance reports to the board, analyze market trends, and recommend improvements.


Performance Metrics


	Fleet utilization rate
	Revenue growth and profitability
	Customer satisfaction scores
	Operational efficiency (downtime reduction, maintenance costs)
	Employee engagement and retention


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in business administration, engineering, or related field (MBA preferred).
	Minimum of 10 years of experience in equipment leasing, construction, or heavy machinery industry, with at least 5 years in a senior management role.
	Proven track record of driving business growth and operational efficiency.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Leadership & People Management: Ability to inspire and manage diverse teams.
	Industry Knowledge: Strong understanding of construction equipment, machinery operations, and leasing practices.
	Financial Acumen: Expertise in budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis.
	Negotiation & Communication: Skilled in contract negotiation, stakeholder engagement, and clear communication.
	Problem-Solving: Ability to handle operational challenges and deliver practical solutions.
	Technology Proficiency: Familiarity with fleet management software, ERP systems, and digital leasing platforms.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using ''General Manager (Construction Equipment & Machinery)'' as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Secretary",
  "description": "Sales Force Consulting (a subsidiary of SF Metrics &amp; Analytics Ltd) is a major recruiting firm with a vision to source highly competent and qualified professionals for companies and professionally-run organizations. Our recruitment services for qualified applicants seeking fresh challenge in well-structured organizations is highly transparent, non-discriminatory and free of any form of charges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Secretary

Job Location: Enugu Ukwu, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time
Interview mode: Virtual and physical

Summary


	The Executive Secretary serves as a senior strategic adviser to The Director, providing deep expertise in non-profit operations, programme strategy, grant mobilization, and stakeholder engagement to guide the Foundation&rsquo;s humanitarian work across Anambra State.
	The role requires a rare blend: executive presence, programme fluency, and commercial acumen applied to a development-sector context where every naira must translate into measurable impact for beneficiaries.


Responsibilities


	Proposal &amp; Grant Strategy: Analyze grant calls, donor concept notes, partnership terms, and MoUs (cost-recovery, matched-funding, performance-based) and formulate a winning positioning for the Foundation&rsquo;s submissions to government agencies, the Bank of Industry, corporate CSR partners, and international donors.
	Strategic Advisory: Advise The Director on emerging frontiers in the development sector maternal and child health innovations, digital inclusion, climate-resilient livelihoods, results-based financing, gender-responsive programming, and disability inclusion.
	Decision Matrices: Prepare programme cost decision matrices, impact dashboards, and value-for-money analyses for high-value interventions including the annual free medical outreach, scholarship schemes, widow-support programmes, and youth empowerment drives.
	Drafting &amp; Review: Draft and review concept notes, programme annexes, safeguarding policies, risk registers, and negotiation strategies with implementing partners.
	Gate-keeping: Act as The Director&rsquo;s strategic gatekeeper, flagging unrealistic programme claims, beneficiary-data integrity concerns, or compliance pitfalls before they reach The Director&rsquo;s desk.
	Representation: Represent The Director in preliminary meetings with the Anambra State Ministries of Health, Education, and Women Affairs, traditional rulers and town unions, religious leaders, medical mission partners, donor agencies, and INGOs.
	Coordination: Coordinate The Director&rsquo;s programme calendar around community outreaches, board engagements, and donor missions, ensuring every engagement advances the Foundation&rsquo;s mission of Service to Humanity.


Academic Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Public Administration, Development Studies, Social Sciences, Public Health, Law, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Minimum 10 years of experience in non-profit, foundation, or development-sector operations, with a proven record of grant-winning and direct executive advisory.
	Master&rsquo;s degree in NGO Management, Development Studies, Public Health, or Public Policy is strongly desirable.


Certifications:


	Project Management Professional (PMP) or PRINCE2.
	Certification in Monitoring &amp; Evaluation (e.g., IPDET, PM4NGOs PMD Pro, or equivalent).
	Specific training in grant management or fundraising (e.g., CFRE, USAID/EU/Global Fund grant frameworks).


Technical &amp; Sectoral Expertise:


	Sector Depth: Minimum 10 years in the humanitarian/development value chain across programme design, delivery, and donor reporting in rural and semi-urban Nigerian settings.
	Development Literacy: Demonstrated understanding of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), Theory of Change, results-based management, safeguarding standards, and gender-responsive programming.
	Digital Transformation: Proficiency in beneficiary-management and field-data platforms (CommCare, KoboToolbox, ODK, Salesforce NPSP, or equivalent) and their impact on reducing programme overheads.
	Regulatory Knowledge: Deep understanding of Nigerian NGO regulations CAC Part F (Incorporated Trustees), SCUML, FIRS NGO tax filings, the NDPA/NDPR, and Anambra State NGO coordination requirements.


Strategic &amp; Soft Skills:


	Programme Modelling: Ability to build and audit programme budgets, cost-per-beneficiary analyses, and value-for-money assessments for major appeals and grant submissions.
	High-Stakes Diplomacy: Proven ability to represent The Director in negotiations with state government, traditional councils (Igwes and town unions), corporate CSR teams, and international donors.
	Risk Foresight: A sixth sense for identifying hidden liabilities in MoUs, partner-led implementations, and community-managed grants.
	Discretion: Unimpeachable confidentiality in handling beneficiary records, board communications, and donor correspondence.


Other Qualities:


	High level organization and planning
	Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
	Strong ethical and moral values.
	Multitasking
	Good time management.
	Hardworking, target oriented, with high level of leadership skills.


Salary
N300,000 - N500,000&nbsp; monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (PDF or Word) to: salesforceconsultingng@gmail.com using &ldquo;Executive Secretary&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note


	Enquiries: Chat Whatsapp 08120796570. (No calls or sending of CV to the number)
	The Foundation is an equal-opportunity employer.
	Suitably qualified candidates including women, persons with disabilities, and persons from underrepresented communities are encouraged to apply.
	Only candidates who meet set criteria shall be shortlisted for invitation.

Executive Secretary Job at Sales Force Consulting




        
    

        
        Sales Force Consulting (a subsidiary of SF Metrics & Analytics Ltd) is a major recruiting firm with a vision to source highly competent and qualified professionals for companies and professionally-run organizations. Our recruitment services for qualified applicants seeking fresh challenge in well-structured organizations is highly transparent, non-discriminatory and free of any form of charges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Secretary

Job Location: Enugu Ukwu, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time
Interview mode: Virtual and physical

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Executive Secretary serves as a senior strategic adviser to The Director, providing deep expertise in non-profit operations, programme strategy, grant mobilization, and stakeholder engagement to guide the Foundation’s humanitarian work across Anambra State.
	The role requires a rare blend: executive presence, programme fluency, and commercial acumen applied to a development-sector context where every naira must translate into measurable impact for beneficiaries.


Responsibilities


	Proposal & Grant Strategy: Analyze grant calls, donor concept notes, partnership terms, and MoUs (cost-recovery, matched-funding, performance-based) and formulate a winning positioning for the Foundation’s submissions to government agencies, the Bank of Industry, corporate CSR partners, and international donors.
	Strategic Advisory: Advise The Director on emerging frontiers in the development sector maternal and child health innovations, digital inclusion, climate-resilient livelihoods, results-based financing, gender-responsive programming, and disability inclusion.
	Decision Matrices: Prepare programme cost decision matrices, impact dashboards, and value-for-money analyses for high-value interventions including the annual free medical outreach, scholarship schemes, widow-support programmes, and youth empowerment drives.
	Drafting & Review: Draft and review concept notes, programme annexes, safeguarding policies, risk registers, and negotiation strategies with implementing partners.
	Gate-keeping: Act as The Director’s strategic gatekeeper, flagging unrealistic programme claims, beneficiary-data integrity concerns, or compliance pitfalls before they reach The Director’s desk.
	Representation: Represent The Director in preliminary meetings with the Anambra State Ministries of Health, Education, and Women Affairs, traditional rulers and town unions, religious leaders, medical mission partners, donor agencies, and INGOs.
	Coordination: Coordinate The Director’s programme calendar around community outreaches, board engagements, and donor missions, ensuring every engagement advances the Foundation’s mission of Service to Humanity.


Academic Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Public Administration, Development Studies, Social Sciences, Public Health, Law, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Minimum 10 years of experience in non-profit, foundation, or development-sector operations, with a proven record of grant-winning and direct executive advisory.
	Master’s degree in NGO Management, Development Studies, Public Health, or Public Policy is strongly desirable.


Certifications:


	Project Management Professional (PMP) or PRINCE2.
	Certification in Monitoring & Evaluation (e.g., IPDET, PM4NGOs PMD Pro, or equivalent).
	Specific training in grant management or fundraising (e.g., CFRE, USAID/EU/Global Fund grant frameworks).


Technical & Sectoral Expertise:


	Sector Depth: Minimum 10 years in the humanitarian/development value chain across programme design, delivery, and donor reporting in rural and semi-urban Nigerian settings.
	Development Literacy: Demonstrated understanding of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), Theory of Change, results-based management, safeguarding standards, and gender-responsive programming.
	Digital Transformation: Proficiency in beneficiary-management and field-data platforms (CommCare, KoboToolbox, ODK, Salesforce NPSP, or equivalent) and their impact on reducing programme overheads.
	Regulatory Knowledge: Deep understanding of Nigerian NGO regulations CAC Part F (Incorporated Trustees), SCUML, FIRS NGO tax filings, the NDPA/NDPR, and Anambra State NGO coordination requirements.


Strategic & Soft Skills:


	Programme Modelling: Ability to build and audit programme budgets, cost-per-beneficiary analyses, and value-for-money assessments for major appeals and grant submissions.
	High-Stakes Diplomacy: Proven ability to represent The Director in negotiations with state government, traditional councils (Igwes and town unions), corporate CSR teams, and international donors.
	Risk Foresight: A sixth sense for identifying hidden liabilities in MoUs, partner-led implementations, and community-managed grants.
	Discretion: Unimpeachable confidentiality in handling beneficiary records, board communications, and donor correspondence.


Other Qualities:


	High level organization and planning
	Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
	Strong ethical and moral values.
	Multitasking
	Good time management.
	Hardworking, target oriented, with high level of leadership skills.


Salary
N300,000 - N500,000  monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (PDF or Word) to: [email protected] using “Executive Secretary" as the subject of the email.

Note


	Enquiries: Chat Whatsapp 08120796570. (No calls or sending of CV to the number)
	The Foundation is an equal-opportunity employer.
	Suitably qualified candidates including women, persons with disabilities, and persons from underrepresented communities are encouraged to apply.
	Only candidates who meet set criteria shall be shortlisted for invitation.
